model.comment: Also marketed as ROCK Pi 4B SE in the official datasheet.

release-date.comment: Went on sale via OKdo on 2022-09-06; datasheet rev 1.2 dated 2022-03-29.

status.comment: Radxa guarantees product availability through September 2029 (datasheet section 6).

open-source-hardware.comment: Radxa's OSHW repo only contains rock_pro and rock2_square_baseboard; no PCB design files for the ROCK 4 family. Schematics are published as PDFs but PCB design files are not, so this does not meet the OSHW Assessment Guideline (PCB design files required).

country-of-origin.comment: Radxa Computer (Shenzhen) Co., Ltd. is the design HQ; published as the copyright holder in the datasheet documentation.

soc.comment: RK3399-T is the lower-clocked variant of RK3399 (1.5 GHz A72 cluster vs 1.8 GHz on full RK3399).

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Cortex-A72 cluster at 1.5 GHz; Cortex-A53 cluster at 1.0 GHz.

process-node.comment: RK3399 family is fabricated on 28 nm HKMG; not stated explicitly in the datasheet but well-documented for the RK3399 SoC line.

vulkan-support.comment: Mali-T860MP4 supports Vulkan 1.0 (vendor BLOB driver). Mainline panfrost driver also exposes Vulkan via PanVK on GLES-class Mali.

npu.comment: RK3399-T has no NPU. (RK3399Pro has one, but this is RK3399-T.)

hw-video-decode.comment: Datasheet: H.265/VP9 (HEVC) up to 4Kp60 hardware decode; H.264 up to 1080p60 hardware decode. No AV1.

hw-video-encode.comment: RK3399 family has hardware H.264 encode (1080p) via VPU. Datasheet does not list explicit encode codecs; recorded conservatively from the RK3399 SoC capabilities.

ram-min-gb.comment: Vendor product page (2026) lists 2/4/8 GB. Note: the original 2022 datasheet rev 1.2 stated 4 GB only. The product page is the current source of truth.

ram-max-gb.comment: Vendor product page (2026) lists 2/4/8 GB SKUs. The 4 GB SKU is by far the most widely retailed.

emmc.comment: Industrial-pinout eMMC socket (modules sold separately, up to 128 GB).

m2-nvme-slot.comment: M.2 M-key NVMe slot on the underside, 1× PCIe 2.x lane (RK3399 PCIe), supports up to 2 TB SSDs per vendor.

sata.comment: No native SATA; storage is microSD / eMMC / M.2 NVMe.

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: 1× USB3 host + 1× USB3 OTG (type-A); a hardware switch toggles the OTG port between host and device modes.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: Single USB-C is power-only (USB PD / QC); no USB-C data port exposed.

poe-support.comment: Onboard 4-pin PoE header for the optional Radxa PoE HAT; PoE not built into the Ethernet port itself.

max-display-resolution.comment: HDMI 2.0 with CEC, up to 4Kp60. Dual display via HDMI + MIPI-DSI (DSI capped at 1080p60).

displayport.comment: No DisplayPort and no USB-C DP-Alt mode (USB-C is power-only on this board).

audio-jack.comment: 4-ring 3.5 mm jack with mic input; 24-bit/96 kHz HD codec.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: 40-pin header laid out compatible with the Raspberry Pi (3.3 V/5 V/GND on the same pins) and matching the Rock Pi 4 family pinout.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Mechanically Pi-shaped (~85×56 mm with Pi-style mounting holes) and electrically Pi-compatible 40-pin header. No HAT EEPROM auto-config standard, but most third-party HATs fit and work.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: M.2 M-key 2280-class slot on the underside, NVMe SSD up to 2 TB; single PCIe 2.x lane from the RK3399.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: Datasheet pin table exposes 2× UART, 2× SPI, 3× I²C, plus PCM/I2S, SPDIF, PWM, and ADC on the 40-pin header.

power-input.comment: USB Type-C power port supports USB PD 2.0, Qualcomm QC 3.0/2.0, or any fixed 6–24 V supply. 5 V from GPIO pins 2/4 also supported.

idle-power-w.comment: No measured idle figure published by Radxa or by a credible reviewer (Phoronix, Jeff Geerling, CNX-Software) specifically for the ROCK 4 SE. Per RESEARCH.md, prefer null over extrapolating from the original Rock Pi 4.

load-power-w.comment: No measured load figure published by Radxa or by a credible reviewer specifically for the ROCK 4 SE. Per RESEARCH.md, prefer null over extrapolating from the original Rock Pi 4.

cooling-required.comment: Datasheet (section 5.7): "will operate perfectly well without any extra cooling … designed for sprint performance." Throttles at 85 °C. Heatsink only needed for sustained high load.

rtc-battery-header.comment: Onboard RTC battery header on the underside (visible in the datasheet board diagram, page 3).

length-mm.comment: Mechanical drawing in datasheet section 3 lists 84.8316 mm (Pi-standard length).

width-mm.comment: Mechanical drawing in datasheet section 3 lists 56.0019 mm (Pi-standard width).

form-factor-class.comment: Within ~5 mm of 85×56 mm and Pi-style mounting holes — matches the Assessment Guideline for pi-standard.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Mounting hole spacing 49.0 × 58.0 mm matches Raspberry Pi 4 / Rock Pi 4 family layout.

weight-g.comment: Radxa does not publish a bare-board weight; retailer pages list ~58 g but that's commonly the gross/packaged weight rather than PCB-only. Recorded null per RESEARCH.md guidance to avoid extrapolation.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Board-specific DTS rk3399-rock-4se.dts merged upstream (arrived in 2023, present in mainline as of v6.6+). RK3399 has mature mainline support (Ethernet, USB3, HDMI, GPIO, GPU via panfrost, eMMC, MMC). Wi-Fi/BT via brcmfmac may need firmware blobs.

official-os-image.comment: Radxa publishes an official Debian Bookworm + KDE image (rock-4se_bookworm_kde) via radxa-build/rock-4se on GitHub.

distros-with-images.comment: Radxa officially publishes Debian. Armbian supports the Rock Pi 4 family broadly (community / WIP for ROCK 4 SE specifically — DTS is upstream so kernel boots, but a board-specific Armbian image was not consistently published as of early 2024). Android images were also published by Radxa for Rock Pi 4 family (Android 7.1/9/10/11) but no longer surface for the SE specifically.

bootloader.comment: Mainline U-Boot has board support ("arm: rockchip: Add Radxa ROCK 4SE" patch series, July 2023).

long-term-support.comment: Datasheet section 6: "ROCK Pi guarantee availability ROCK Pi 4B SE until at least September 2029" — 7-year window from 2022 launch.

android-support.comment: Datasheet lists Android 7.1 / 9.0 / 10 / 11 support. Maintenance status of Android images today is unclear; original SDK images existed at launch.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Launch price (Sept 2022) approximately US$70 for the 4 GB SKU via OKdo. Radxa does not publish a public MSRP page; this is the OKdo launch price (£61.12 ex-VAT ≈ $70.50). Checked 2026-04-26.

msrp-top-usd.comment: 8 GB SKU is listed by Radxa but no consistent public MSRP found; retailers vary widely. 4 GB is by far the most-retailed SKU. Checked 2026-04-26.

available-regions.comment: Sold via OKdo (global), ameriDroid (US), DFRobot (Asia/global), SB Components (UK), Arduino Store (EU), Rokland (US). Snapshot 2026-04-26.

typical-lead-time.comment: In stock at major resellers (ameriDroid, OKdo, DFRobot) as of 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Adequate: official PDF datasheet (rev 1.2), wiki page, and docs portal exist; schematics PDF available; coverage is decent but inconsistent (older wiki vs newer docs portal split, no full PCB design files, sparse troubleshooting guides for the SE specifically).

community-size.comment: Radxa is an established second-tier vendor per RESEARCH.md thresholds; ROCK 4 SE is one of many boards in the line and inherits the wider Rock Pi 4 community.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run forum (forum.radxa.com) with regular activity, including a dedicated ROCK 4 Series subforum.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Adequate: official PoE HAT, M.2 cases, multiple third-party cases (KKSB, GeeekPi, 52Pi), heatsinks, fans. Pi-style header lets most generic HATs fit but no SE-specific HAT certification programme.
